game_name: Low G Man: The Low Gravity Man

danger_signals.visual_form: small enemy sprites positioned in level spaces
danger_signals.meaning: obstacles requiring freeze-ray or high-jump avoidance tactics

danger_signals.visual_form: large boss sprites at stage conclusions
danger_signals.meaning: major threat requiring sustained combat and weapon strategy

danger_signals.visual_form: science fiction industrial aesthetic with futuristic color palette
danger_signals.meaning: external existential threat—alien force has conquered robot-manufacturing planet
temporary vulnerability requiring immediate melee follow-up (top/bottom spear stab) before thaw

safety_signals.visual_form: dropped power-up objects after enemy defeat
safety_signals.meaning: reward for high-jump kills (no freeze-ray) provides resource replenishment

safety_signals.visual_form: password screen UI with alphabet/consonant grid
safety_signals.meaning: save state persistence—progression is recoverable after game-over
predictable advancement structure with compartmentalized challenges

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: high-jumping humanoid with freeze-ray and spear weapon
small varied enemy sprites; large boss forms
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: uninteresting standard enemies with distinct large boss variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: occupy planet stages; represent external conquest force
planet setting with tech-forward visual architecture; implied conquered state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: science fiction industrial aesthetic pervades level design; futuristic color palette dominates environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies actively patrol/occupy stages; boss encounters structure progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: multiple chapters (stages) with progressive difficulty and enemy variety
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player forced to adapt weapon strategy (freeze-ray vs. high-jump kill tactics) per level design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: visible weapon inventory (freeze-ray, fireballs, boomerangs, force-waves, bombs)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: stage design demands switching between freeze-and-stab and alternative weapon tactics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: protagonist capable of three-screen-height jumps; level architecture accommodates extreme vertical traversal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: jerky horizontal scrolling; high-jump mechanics enable enemy bypass or aerial damage

environmental_storytelling.example: robot-manufacturing planet with industrial futuristic aesthetic communicates civilization type and economic context of conquered world
variety of alien enemy sprites paired with distinctive large bosses indicates organized invasion force with hierarchical command
chapter-based structure with chapter numbers suggests systematic clearing of alien-held sectors
freeze-ray primary weapon (non-lethal) paired with spear finisher suggests technological asymmetry or strategic doctrine

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: floating island with two heads sprite (cut content)
environmental_storytelling.example: indicates intended narrative beats or environmental world-building elements removed in final build—suggests richer lore backdrop

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: positioned in level spaces; uninteresting movement patterns per reviewer notes
becomes immobilized; requires top/bottom spear stab to defeat; generates power-ups on high-jump kills only
large sprite form; distinct visual identity; requires sustained combat strategy
capable of three-screen-height jumps; enables vertical level navigation and aerial enemy damage
freeze-ray disables targets; alternative weapons (fireballs, boomerangs, force-waves, bombs) provide tactical variety

notes: Low G Man communicates world-state through science-fiction industrial visual language combined with alien invasion narrative framing. Danger is signaled through enemy and boss sprite presence; safety through power-up rewards and password checkpointing. The low-gravity mechanic is the primary visual/behavioral world-state indicator—three-screen jumps reshape level architecture perception and enemy avoidance tactics. Factions are implicitly communicated: protagonist (human defender), aliens (invaders), and robot civilization (conquered). No explicit political negotiation mechanics; world-state expressed through aesthetic and challenge structure rather than faction reputation systems. Unused floating-island cutscene imagery suggests deeper narrative lore was planned but cut. Regional password-screen differences (US: full alphabet + heart; EU: consonants/numbers) indicate minor localization variance but no faction/state differentiation.
